value.Title: Nguyen's research drives agricultural solutions from her hometown to the world

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JH5-BR33-RV78-618W-00000-00Nguyen's research drives agricultural solutions from her hometown to the world2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:51:20ZLexisNexisNEWSNguyen's research drives agricultural solutions from her hometown to the worldBradley Nading

BY MALORIE SOUGÉY

K-State News and Communications Services

MANHATTAN Agriculture has been a part of Vivian Nguyen's life for as long as she can remember. It was a backdrop to daily life as she grew up in Dodge City, from the scenery of open crop fields to cattle feedlots on the edge of town.

"I didn't pay that much attention to agriculture then," Nguyen said, "but looking back, it was always around me."

Now a sophomore studying mechanical engineering and physics at Kansas State University, Nguyen is conducting research to address agricultural issues that affect people in her hometown, throughout Kansas and across the world.

In her latest research, she works alongside Suprem Das, associate professor of industrial and manufacturing systems engineering, to explore ways to detect phosphorus which can affect soil, crop growth and water quality by using electrochemical sensors.

Nguyen's academic and research excellence have been recognized on a national level, and as K-State's 84th Goldwater scholar, her drive to tackle real-world challenges through hands-on research is at an all-time high.

Goldwater scholar's undergraduate research experience spans STEM fields

Through participation in K-State's Developing Scholars Program in the Scholar Development and Undergraduate Research unit, Nguyen has conducted five distinct mentored research projects to date.

Before connecting with Das, she worked with mathematics graduate student Hong Nhung Nguyen to analyze numerical methods for accuracy and error, and she studied mathematical methods for reconstructing point-source locations using imaging functions with Professor Liem Nguyen.

She also explored phase separation in biomolecules with Professor Jeremy Schmit, physics, and conducted research about renewable energy microgrid systems with Professor Hongyu Wu, electrical and computer engineering.

"I have a variety of research experiences because I wanted to see all the different research areas in engineering, physics and mathematics," Nguyen said. "I added a mechanical engineering degree in my second semester when I realized I love the application of science."

That passion for application-based research is what led her to Das and his work in electrochemical sensing.

Electrochemical sensing research project connects to Nguyen's rural roots

Nguyen began her research with Das by exploring nanomaterials for electrochemical sensing, which taught her about how engineering design and materials processing are used to develop devices for real-world chemical detection.

She eventually focused on MXene-based electrochemical sensors for phosphate detection. MXene is a highly conductive nanomaterial, and sensors made with it detect phosphate by measuring changes in electrical signals.

"Phosphate is used in fertilizers, but when there is an excess amount, it can wash into water, causing harmful algal blooms that kill fish and make water unsafe," Nguyen said. "In addition, too much phosphate in soil can negatively impact crop growth. Detecting it early helps prevent environmental damage, especially in agricultural states like Kansas."

The project involves hands-on lab work like coating electrodes with MXene, placing them in solutions and running experiments to measure electrical responses. Nguyen uses a potentiostat and a three-electrode setup for the research. If someone happened across her in the lab, they'd see her pipetting solutions, polishing electrodes, connecting wires and analyzing graphs.

Nguyen said the research will help prevent water pollution and support safer farming practices, and it could even lead to farmers using real-time sensors to protect drinking water, ecosystems and plant growth.

"The research I'm doing connects directly back to where I'm from, she said. "Growing up in Dodge City, agriculture was just part of everyday life, but I never thought about its impact on water. Now, being able to work on research that addresses those exact issues makes everything feel connected.

"It reminds me that what I'm learning isn't just for school, it's something I can use to give back to my community and make a real impact."

Nguyen earns Goldwater scholarship for academic and research excellence

Through her research efforts spanning physics, engineering and math, Nguyen has developed a research portfolio worthy of national recognition.

The Barry Goldwater Scholarship and Excellence in Education Foundation named Nguyen a 2026 Goldwater scholar. She is one of only 452 undergraduates nationwide to receive the honor, out of nearly 1,500 total applicants.

Established by Congress in 1986 to honor Sen. Barry M. Goldwater of Arizona, the scholarship recognizes students who are exceptionally active in research and intend to pursue careers in mathematics, science or engineering. Awardees receive up to $7,500 annually for college-related expenses.

In addition to her impressive research portfolios, Nguyen is also an active member of campus organizations, including the University Honors Program, Engineering Ambassadors, Silver Key Sophomore Honorary and the Tri-Alpha Honor Society.

Her other honors include the Pattie Johnson Wilson Scholarship, the Mortar Board Outstanding Student Scholarship, the Semester Scholastic Award and the Above and Beyond Award.

"Receiving the Goldwater Scholarship is especially meaningful to me as a first-generation student," Nguyen said. "I first learned about this opportunity as a freshman through my work with Scholar Development and Undergraduate Research, so it feels really full circle to now be receiving it. This award supports my goal of pursuing a Ph.D. in materials science and engineering and continuing research that develops practical solutions to real-world problems."

In another full-circle moment, Nguyen will be working with Koch Fertilizer in Dodge City this summer as a reliability engineering intern.

"Vivian has seized opportunities at K-State, such as the Developing Scholars Program and summer NSF research, that have allowed her to explore research in math, physics and engineering," Beth Powers, director of Scholar Development and Undergraduate Research at K-State, said. "The range of research she has engaged with has built valuable knowledge and skills and given her the chance to explore work in various STEM settings as she decides where she would like her career to take her."

"We are excited to see where her curiosity and drive take her next."

CUTLINE

Vivian Nguyen, from Dodge City and now a student at K-State, has been named a 2026 Goldwater scholar. COURTESY

"Receiving the Goldwater Scholarship is especially meaningful to me as a first-generation student," Nguyen said. This award supports my goal of pursuing a Ph.D. in materials science and engineering and continuing research that develops practical solutions to real-world problems."

value.Title: Sierra Club , Allies Applaud Approval of Accelergen 's East Side Energy Storage for Wyandotte County

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JH0-1G23-RYJY-T2RT-00000-00Sierra Club, Allies Applaud Approval of Accelergen's East Side Energy Storage for Wyandotte County2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:51:20ZLexisNexisSierra Club, Allies Applaud Approval of Accelergen's East Side Energy Storage for Wyandotte County

Key Highlights:

* 10-year conditional use permit granted with extension opportunities.

* New local jobs, increased tax revenue, and energy cost control expected.

* Community engagement and partnerships will benefit from the project.

Original Press Release:

KANSAS CITY, Kansas, May 1 -- Sierra Club issued the following news release:

Last night, the Unified Government of Wyandotte County voted 8-2 to approve Accelergen's proposed East Side Energy Storage project, which at 300 MW when complete will be among the newest and largest utility-scale energy storage systems in Kansas.

"Energy storage is the keystone of renewable power, bridging the gap between electricity produced by wind and solar, and delivering it to homes like yours when it's needed most. As we all feel the squeeze of high energy prices, this project will ease that burden and ensure that we can rely on more clean, cheap energy produced right here in our backyard." said Ty Gorman, Sr. Campaign Organizing Strategist for Sierra Club's Beyond Coal Campaign.

"The Climate + Energy Project applauds Wyandotte County local leaders for their support of energy storage. Last night's vote provides lasting financial benefits for county residents while also providing important energy benefits for Kansas City Board of Public Utility customers," says Dorothy Barnett of Climate + Energy Project.

With nearly twice as many supporters as opponents at last night's meeting, the Commission approved a 10 year conditional use permit with extension opportunities for Accelergen's battery storage. Along with the Sierra Club, Climate + Energy Project, and Kansas Latino Community Network played an active role in engaging and educating the public and ensuring commissioners heard from their constituents before the vote. Labor groups like IBEW and AFL-CIO were also involved in support of the project. Accelergen took proactive steps to capture public support too, a move that advocates applauded throughout the process.

After the Planning and Zoning Commission initially recommended against approval, this final result reflects the value of community engagement in public processes, responsive development practices, and thoughtful leadership. Long-term benefits of the project will include new local jobs, increased tax revenue to support essential services, increased control over future energy costs, community partnerships benefits, and enhanced energy reliability.

About the Sierra Club

The Sierra Club is America's largest and most influential grassroots environmental organization, with millions of members and supporters. In addition to protecting every person's right to get outdoors and access the healing power of nature, the Sierra Club works to promote clean energy, safeguard the health of our communities, protect wildlife, and preserve our remaining wild places through grassroots activism, public education, lobbying, and legal action. For more information, visit www.sierraclub.org.

[Category: Energy, Renewable Energy, ESG and Sustainability, Sustainability Initiatives, CSR]

Source: Sierra Club

value.Title: Multiple 'hyperscale' data centers in the works around KC. What that means, see map
value.Overview: The Kansas City Star (Missouri), KANSAS CITY NEWS, 580words

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJF-BB33-S4BM-D3KS-00000-00Multiple 'hyperscale' data centers in the works around KC. What that means, see map2026-05-11T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:45:59ZLexisNexisKANSAS CITY NEWSMultiple 'hyperscale' data centers in the works around KC. What that means, see mapSofi Zeman

As data center projects sprout up across the Kansas City metropolitan area, so are residents' concerns over what that means for their health and natural resources.

How much area water do they use? How will this new wave of development affect energy demand and prices? What are the risks of building something that relies on burning fossil fuels?

Those questions - which have been raised in local rooms from Independence to Smithville to Spring Hill - come at a time when technological advancements and Donald Trump's presidency have altered the way the U.S. Environmental Protection Agency regulates industry.

The number of data centers - facilities used to house computer systems and optimize internet efficiency - are surging in the United States as new tech gains momentum and companies become increasingly reliant on artificial intelligence.

Numerous data center projects that are eclipsing the size of older models are planned for the next few years in the KC metro, and some are already operating.

On both sides of the state line, in Kansas and Missouri , legislators have signaled that they want to encourage data center development through tax breaks designed for the facilities.

While national-level organizations are suing the EPA in an effort to get stronger federal safeguards, some local advocates around KC are calling on community and state officials to push developers to operate using clean energy sources alone.

Hyperscale data centers

Data center companies are in a global race to produce more AI, faster. And although some have pledged to lean into renewable energy, many rely on increasingly-strained power grids and fossil fuel-powered turbines to get ahead. Energy demand, thus the amount of combustion or other sources required to operate a data center, depends on scale.

More and more often, new data center projects are being described as hyperscale. That's a term that has slightly different definitions depending on what side of the state line someone's on. Kansas classifies projects as hyperscale if they use more than 75 megawatts of energy to operate. Legislation proposed in Missouri's defined hyperscale data centers as using more than 25 megawatts . Evergy defines "large load" projects as exceeding 75 megawatts.

Of the at least 10 large data center project proposals pending in the Kansas City metro, at least five are planned to operate using 100 or more megawatts, according to project proposals, company and city representatives and data compiled from Data Center Map, a site that has tracked such projects, pending and existing, since 2007.

Companies like Meta, which has a center operating in the Northland, have said they plan to match their data center use using entirely clean and renewable energy. Nebius, which is building a large, at least 800 MW facility out in Independence, will utilize some renewable energy while also leaning heavily on fossil fuel turbines.

Hyperscale data centers require notably higher amounts of power to operate than smaller facilities around the metro.

They also need more backup generators.

Data centers use emergency backup generators for reliability purposes, like during grid failures. Those backup generators, often powered using combustion turbines, are associated with pollution in the form of carbon monoxide, nitrogen oxides, volatile organic compounds, particulate matter and some hazardous air pollutants, according to the EPA.

See the map of which larger data centers are up and running, and which are coming.

value.Title: Evergy Breaks Ground on New Natural Gas Plant in Sumner County

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JK3-D173-RWWB-R4D2-00000-00Evergy Breaks Ground on New Natural Gas Plant in Sumner County2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:51:20ZLexisNexisEvergy Breaks Ground on New Natural Gas Plant in Sumner County

AMERICAN DAILY STOCK REPORT

UNITED STATES EDITION

14 May 2026 08:15 EDT

Evergy, Inc. has officially commenced construction on a new 710-megawatt combined-cycle natural gas plant in Sumner County. The groundbreaking ceremony, held on 12th May 2026, was attended by state lawmakers, local officials, and Evergy leadership, highlighting the project's significance for job creation and regional energy reliability.

The facility, named the Chisholm Trail Energy Center, is expected to begin operations in 2029. Evergy's chairman and CEO, David Campbell, described the project as a crucial infrastructure investment to meet the growing energy demands of the region. This marks the first new baseload power plant added to Evergy's Kansas generation fleet in over four decades.

The Chisholm Trail Energy Center is strategically located in northwest Sumner County and will utilize advanced combined-cycle technology. This modern approach is designed not only to provide a reliable and steady supply of electricity but also to adapt its output based on the availability of renewable energy sources like wind and solar, thereby ensuring competitiveness in electricity pricing.

In addition to meeting immediate energy needs, the plant is projected to create approximately 500 jobs during its construction phase and maintain 20 to 40 long-term positions, with salaries exceeding $90,000 annually. The investment is expected to generate over $500 million in property tax revenues for Sumner County, further supporting local economic growth.

Evergy continues to expand its energy portfolio, which currently incorporates coal, natural gas, nuclear, wind, and solar generation. The Chisholm Trail Energy Center is part of a broader strategy to enhance energy infrastructure in Kansas, ensuring that both families and businesses have access to reliable and affordable power well into the future.

SECTION 2 EVERGY ACTIVITIES

Activities

Evergy Inc., headquartered in Kansas, is a prominent electric utility company serving approximately 1.6 million customers across Kansas and Missouri. Its core business centers on the generation, transmission, distribution, and sale of electricity through a diversified energy portfolio that includes coal, natural gas, nuclear, and renewable sources. The company plays a vital role in regional energy reliability and sustainability, with a strong emphasis on modernizing its infrastructure to enhance efficiency and reduce emissions. Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy initiatives, particularly wind and solar projects, which have expanded rapidly due to increasing demand for clean power and supportive regulatory policies. This strategic focus positions Evergy to capitalize on the transition to a low-carbon economy, supporting long-term growth and innovation in the utility sector. It is the NASDAQ's largest Publishing company by market capitalisation.

SECTION 2 CORPORATE PROFILE

2.1 Activities

Evergy Inc., headquartered in Kansas, is a prominent electric utility company serving approximately 1.6 million customers across Kansas and Missouri. Its core business centers on the generation, transmission, distribution, and sale of electricity through a diversified energy portfolio that includes coal, natural gas, nuclear, and renewable sources. The company plays a vital role in regional energy reliability and sustainability, with a strong emphasis on modernizing its infrastructure to enhance efficiency and reduce emissions. Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy initiatives, particularly wind and solar projects, which have expanded rapidly due to increasing demand for clean power and supportive regulatory policies. This strategic focus positions Evergy to capitalize on the transition to a low-carbon economy, supporting long-term growth and innovation in the utility sector. It is the NASDAQ's largest Publishing company by market capitalisation.

Evergy Inc (NASDAQ:EVRG) strategically positions itself as a leading utility in grid modernization with a formidable $2.8B grid spend. The company's focus on Evergy grid investment and Evergy data center demand underscores its commitment to infrastructure improvements. The Evergy CEO highlights the company's robust plan for Evergy capital expenditure, aimed at enhancing Evergy grid resilience through smart grid solutions. Evergy's infrastructure projects, including transmission upgrades and distribution upgrades, ensure grid reliability while fostering Evergy renewable energy integration. The firm capitalizes on Evergy commercial data center solutions by supporting Evergy data center power needs and interconnections. This is reflected in their growing Evergy data center load growth. Moreover, Evergy's utility infrastructure spending catalyzes Evergy rate base growth, enhancing shareholder value. Regular EVRG investor updates, where shareholders re-elect directors and approve pay, emphasize strong corporate governance. Evergy investor relations and regulated utility investments secure its position as a notable Evergy utility stock EVRG in the market.

SECTION 3 RECENT NEWS, PAST YEAR TOP 10 STORIES & PAST MONTH ECONOMIC NEWS

3.1 RECENT NEWS

Recent News 1 of 2, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 369 words

Fri May 8, 2026: Evergy Inc. Projects 8% Growth in Retail Sales Amid Data Center Boom

Evergy Inc. is forecasting retail sales growth of 7% to 8% annually through 2030, a revision from its previous estimate of 6%. This optimistic outlook is largely attributed to the expansion of data centers in its service area. The announcement was made during a quarterly earnings conference call on 8th May 2026, where company officials discussed the impact of large-load electric service agreements (ESAs) on their revenue.

During the call, Evergy's chairman, president, and CEO, David Campbell, revealed that the utility has signed a new ESA with a yet-to-be-named customer. This brings the total demand from large load ESAs to 2.5 GW, a significant increase from the 1.9 GW disclosed in the previous quarter. Campbell also indicated that the company is in talks to secure at least one more ESA with a large customer within the year, underlining the growing demand for electricity in the region.

The utility company has already contracted an additional 450 MW with major customers, including Panasonic's electric vehicle battery manufacturing plant. Furthermore, Campbell noted that discussions are underway to expand existing ESAs by up to 1.5 GW and to bring in 1.5 GW to 3 GW of new large-load customers post-2030. This potential growth reflects the proactive steps Evergy is taking to enhance its infrastructure in response to increased demand.

In the first quarter of 2026, Evergy reported a modest increase in income, rising 21% to $151.5 million, or 64 cents per share, compared to $125 million, or 54 cents per share, in the same period a year prior. Despite flat revenue of $1.4 billion, the company attributed its income growth to a rate increase in Kansas, higher non-regulated energy marketing revenue, and a reduction in tax expenses. However, rising depreciation and maintenance costs were also noted as factors impacting overall profitability.

To accommodate the growing demand, Evergy has adjusted its planned capital investment strategy, now projecting a capital expenditure of $21.6 billion. This entails increasing gas-fired generation in Missouri to 4.7 GW, while scaling back long-term renewable energy plans by over 90%. The revised strategy comes in response to challenges related to renewable development costs and regulatory changes, indicating a shift in focus as the company navigates the evolving landscape of energy demand.

Recent News 2 of 2, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 338 words

Tue May 5, 2026: Evergy Inc. Directors Re-Elected Amid $2.8B Grid Investment

Evergy Inc. held its Annual Shareholder Meeting on 5th May 2026, where shareholders voted to re-elect all 12 directors, approve the advisory vote on 2025 executive compensation, and ratify Deloitte & Touche as the company's independent auditor for the upcoming year. Heather Humphrey, Senior Vice President, General Counsel, and Corporate Secretary, noted that the record date for voting was 2nd March 2026 and confirmed a quorum was present for the meeting.

During the meeting, Humphrey reported on the voting results, stating that all director nominees were successfully elected, and the executive compensation proposal was approved. Additionally, the appointment of Deloitte & Touche was ratified. The re-election of the board comes as Evergy moves forward with significant investments in infrastructure, aimed at improving both reliability and service for its customers across Kansas and Missouri.

CEO David Campbell highlighted that Evergy invested $2.8 billion in 2025 to modernize its grid and enhance reliability, capitalizing on a burgeoning demand from advanced manufacturing sectors and data centers. He noted that the economic development pipeline has expanded to over 15 gigawatts, supported by new electric service agreements with major data center clients totaling 1.9 gigawatts.

Campbell also discussed Evergy's plans for growth through the construction of three natural gas plants and three solar farms, which will add nearly 2.2 gigawatts of capacity. These projects have received the necessary approvals from state regulatory bodies and are complemented by new large load power service tariffs aimed at ensuring cost recovery and affordability for existing customers. The recent passage of Missouri's Senate Bill 4 further supports Evergy's infrastructure investment strategy.

As part of its commitment to returning value to shareholders, Evergy announced a 4% increase in its annual dividend, raising it to $2.78 per share. Campbell emphasized that the company remains focused on translating opportunities into growth while maintaining a strong commitment to customer service, reliability, and safety. The meeting concluded with an invitation for investors to participate in the upcoming first-quarter earnings call, where further details on financial performance and strategic initiatives will be discussed.

3.2 PAST YEAR TOP 10 STORIES

Story 1 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 80 words

Evergy Forges Advanced Nuclear Partnership With TerraPower and Kansas

Evergy, in partnership with TerraPower and the Kansas Department of Commerce, signed a memorandum of understanding to explore siting a Natrium advanced nuclear reactor in Kansas. The project, backed by significant private financing, aims to add 345-500 MW of carbon-free baseload generation to meet growing industrial demand. While the agreement positions Evergy as a leader in next-generation nuclear technology, it faces substantial risks related to regulatory approval, construction costs, and public acceptance, with a project timeline likely extending beyond 2030.

Story 2 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 71 words

Evergy Unveils $17.5 Billion Capital Investment Program Through 2029

Evergy announced a $17.5 billion five-year capital investment plan to accommodate unprecedented demand growth, largely from data centers. The program allocates funds for new natural gas and solar generation, transmission upgrades, and grid modernization, targeting an 8.5% annualized rate base growth. The plan will be financed through cash flow, debt, and equity, which may dilute existing shares. Execution depends on managing risks like cost inflation, supply chain issues, and regulatory approvals.

Story 3 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 69 words

Kansas Regulators Approve $128 Million Rate Increase Settlement

The Kansas Corporation Commission approved a $128 million annual revenue increase for Evergy Kansas Central, a significant reduction from the initial $196.4 million request. The settlement, reached with multiple stakeholders, balances infrastructure investment needs with customer affordability, resulting in an average monthly bill increase of $8.47 for residential customers. This outcome provides regulatory clarity and facilitates cost recovery for grid modernization projects, demonstrating a constructive but disciplined regulatory environment.

Story 4 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 68 words

Regulators Approve Landmark Tariffs for Data Center Power Supply

Regulators in Missouri and Kansas approved specialized tariffs for large-load customers like data centers, removing a key barrier to securing contracts for new projects. The tariffs establish rates 7-10% above existing industrial prices, require long-term commitments, and hold customers responsible for their interconnection costs. The structure ensures existing ratepayers are not subsidizing new growth and includes clean energy options, making Evergy's service area more attractive for hyperscale development.

Story 5 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 66 words

Evergy Reports Q3 Results, Raises Quarterly Dividend 4 Percent

Evergy reported third-quarter GAAP earnings of $2.03 per share and announced a 4% quarterly dividend increase to $0.6950 per share. Despite 2% weather-normalized demand growth, mild weather conditions led management to narrow its full-year 2025 EPS guidance to $3.92-$4.02. The results reflect the conflict between strong underlying demand and headwinds from interest expenses and regulatory lag, while the dividend hike signals confidence in future cash flow.

Story 6 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 74 words

Evergy Secures Regulatory Approval for 2,184 MW Generation Expansion

Kansas and Missouri regulators approved Evergy's plans to add 2,184 MW of new generation capacity, including two combined-cycle gas plants, one simple-cycle gas plant, and a solar facility. These projects, slated for operation between 2026 and 2030, are designed to meet projected load growth and facilitate the retirement of coal plants. The approvals provide regulatory certainty for construction, though the company faces risks related to cost inflation, fuel price volatility, and supply chain constraints.

Story 7 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 74 words

Lambda Announces $500 Million AI Infrastructure Investment in Evergy Territory

AI cloud provider Lambda announced a $500 million investment to develop an AI Factory in Kansas City, Missouri, repurposing a dormant data center. The facility will launch in 2026 with 24 MW of capacity, scaling beyond 100 MW and featuring high-density NVIDIA GPUs. This project validates Evergy's strategy to attract large-load customers and strengthens the case for its significant capital investments, diversifying its data center client base beyond existing commitments from Meta and Google.

Story 8 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 71 words

Evergy Economic Development Pipeline Surges Past 15 Gigawatts

Evergy's economic development pipeline has grown to over 15 GW, with 4-6 GW of high-probability large-load opportunities, primarily from the data center sector. This potential demand, which could double the utility's current peak load, is the driving force behind its $17.5 billion capital program. While the pipeline validates the company's growth strategy, significant execution risk remains, as converting these opportunities into energized loads depends on navigating complex development and regulatory stages.

Story 9 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 65 words

Evergy Deploys Advanced eSIM Technology to Enhance Grid Resilience

Evergy is deploying Kigen's eSIM technology to improve grid resilience by enabling secure, automated failover between its private LTE network and public cellular networks. This cybersecurity-focused initiative supports grid modernization by ensuring continuous connectivity for smart meters and IoT devices. The technology enhances reliability and supports advanced applications like dynamic billing, representing a foundational investment in long-term operational capability rather than a near-term earnings driver.

Story 10 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 67 words

Edison Electric Institute Recognizes Evergy for Emergency Response Excellence

Evergy received a national Emergency Response Award from the Edison Electric Institute for its outstanding restoration efforts during a Memorial Day storm and its mutual assistance to AEP Kentucky after Hurricane Helene. The award recognizes operational excellence in restoring power, coordinating resources, and ensuring safety. While the recognition has limited direct financial impact, it validates the utility's operational competence and strengthens its reputation with regulators and customers.

3.3 ECONOMIC NEWS

May 8, 2026: AEP's $78 Billion Plan Frames SPP Capex Backdrop for Evergy

American Electric Power (Nasdaq: AEP) disclosed on May 5, 2026 an expansion of its five-year capital plan to $78 billion from $72 billion, alongside an upward revision of its incremental load forecast to 63 gigawatts by 2030. Approximately 90% of that projected demand is associated with hyperscaler and data center customers, according to company disclosures. Following 7 GW of new large-load agreements executed in the first quarter, concentrated in Ohio and Texas, the company indicated that AEP Texas alone accounts for 41 GW of incremental commitments, with Texas Senate Bill 6 expected to enhance interconnection certainty.

The expanded capital program is anchored by newly approved transmission investments in PJM and SPP, complemented by new natural gas-fired generation in Indiana. The company also indicated line of sight to more than $10 billion in additional investment beyond the current plan, and disclosed cost offsets of up to $16 billion for existing customers driven by signed customer agreements.

The announcement reflects how AI-driven data center load is reshaping regulated utility capital programs across the PJM, SPP, and ERCOT footprints.

Impact on Evergy: EVRG

Following this announcement, Evergy faces sharper investor focus on its own data center pipeline in Kansas and Missouri. Evergy operates within SPP, the same RTO where AEP is expanding transmission investments, and has secured large-load commitments including the multi-gigawatt Panasonic battery plant in Kansas alongside emerging data center developments. AEP's $78 billion capital plan and 63 GW pipeline raise the bar for peer utility growth narratives, particularly for utilities operating in SPP where transmission expansion is a shared theme.

Evergy's recently revised integrated resource plans across Kansas Central and Missouri West reflect new gas-fired generation and grid modernization investments to serve incremental load. The company's regulatory framework with the Kansas Corporation Commission and the Missouri Public Service Commission will be tested as it seeks pre-approvals for capital recovery on data center investments, a process AEP has been navigating across multiple commissions.

Supply chain competition for SPP transmission projects and gas turbine deliveries will also affect Evergy as AEP scales its own SPP investments.

Apr 22, 2026: Anterix Closes Benton PUD Private Wireless Sale, Expanding Utility Private-LTE Adoption Base Relevant to Evergy's Grid Modernization Plan

On April 21, 2026, Anterix Inc., through PDV Spectrum Holding Company, LLC, entered into a spectrum license sale agreement with Public Utility District No. 1 of Benton County for a 10 MHz 900 MHz broadband license in Washington state, valued at $771,000. According to industry reports, the utility-owned network will serve more than 58,000 customers across a 927-square-mile territory.

Impact on Evergy: EVRG

Operational metrics indicate Evergy serves approximately 1.7 million customers and has identified private wireless as foundational to its Sustainability Transformation Plan, which includes advanced metering, distribution automation, and distributed energy resource integration.

Analysis shows the Benton PUD agreement adds another deployed utility to the 900 MHz ecosystem, reinforcing vendor scale and standardized integration practices relevant to Evergy's rollout. The growing deployment base also strengthens the regulatory record before Kansas and Missouri commissions overseeing capital recovery.

May 12 2026: KCC Directs Evergy to Reroute Transmission Line for Flint Hills Preservation

WICHITA, Kan. — Evergy Inc has been ordered to identify a new route for a section of its 133-mile transmission line after the Kansas Corporation Commission (KCC) voted on the matter on 13th May 2026. The directive aims to prevent disruption to the Flint Hills ecosystem, an area known for its unique environmental characteristics.

The transmission project, named the Buffalo Flats transmission line, is designed to stretch from the Buffalo Flats substation in Garden Plain, Kansas, to the Kansas/Oklahoma state line west of Caney. An additional 30 miles of transmission line is planned to extend into Delaware, Oklahoma. However, the KCC's unanimous decision mandates Evergy to conduct a new routing study for the segment that traverses the Flint Hills, particularly as it crosses U.S. Highway 77 north of Arkansas City.

In its ruling, the KCC expressed a preference for a route that would run parallel to U.S. Route 166, emphasizing the need for Evergy to submit a revised plan by 12th June 2026. The commission has approved the segment west of U.S. 77, contingent upon Evergy engaging with local homeowners and oil and gas lease operators to address potential disruptions to existing energy lines.

The KCC's decision was influenced by findings that the transmission line would enhance the reliability of the energy network in both Kansas and Missouri. It is also expected to alleviate overload issues in southeastern Kansas, particularly during severe winter weather. Despite these benefits, concerns were raised during public hearings in January, including objections from local farmers about the operational challenges posed by additional transmission poles.

Opposition to the project has been organized by a group named 'Protect the Prairie. Stop the Line,' which has highlighted the potential threats to the Flint Hills as a primary reason for their resistance. As Evergy works to comply with the KCC's order, the future of the Buffalo Flats transmission line remains uncertain, with environmental considerations and community feedback at the forefront of the discussion.

May 12 2026: Evergy Inc. Maintains 2026 EPS Guidance Amid Retail Load Growth Projections

Evergy Inc. has reaffirmed its adjusted earnings per share (EPS) guidance for 2026, projecting a range of $4.14 to $4.34. This announcement was made during the company's earnings call, where CEO David Campbell emphasized the solid performance in the first quarter of 2026. The date of this call was 12th May 2026, during which management reported adjusted earnings of $162 million or $0.69 per share, an increase from $0.55 per share in the same quarter last year.

The reaffirmation of the EPS guidance aligns with Evergy's long-term growth strategy, which includes a target of 7% to 8% annual retail load growth through 2030. Campbell noted that the signing of a fifth large customer electric service agreement, along with favorable amendments to two existing contracts, is expected to boost the company's margins for the year. This growth is particularly important as it will help mitigate the impact of mild winter weather experienced earlier in the year.

Evergy's strategy includes a focus on large load power service tariffs, where new significant customers pay a premium rate that reflects their share of existing and new system costs. The company has executed electric service agreements for five data center projects, resulting in a steady-state peak load of approximately 2.5 gigawatts, bringing the total to about 3 gigawatts. Campbell described this demand as a transformative growth opportunity that supports the revised retail load growth estimate.

In addition to reaffirming the 2026 EPS guidance, Evergy provided insights into its outlook for the second quarter, projecting adjusted EPS growth of 17% to 19% compared to the $4.24 midpoint of its 2026 guidance range. Executive Vice President and CFO W. Buckler highlighted the solid start to the year and expressed confidence in the company's ability to achieve its growth targets, which include an adjusted EPS growth exceeding 8% annually from 2028 through 2030.

As Evergy Inc. continues to navigate the evolving energy landscape, its commitment to maintaining a robust financial outlook and pursuing strategic growth initiatives positions the company favorably for the future. Investors will be watching closely as Evergy implements its plans to expand its retail load and enhance shareholder value.

May 12 2026: Evergy Breaks Ground on New Natural Gas Plant in Sumner County

Evergy, Inc. has officially commenced construction on a new 710-megawatt combined-cycle natural gas plant in Sumner County. The groundbreaking ceremony, held on 12th May 2026, was attended by state lawmakers, local officials, and Evergy leadership, highlighting the project's significance for job creation and regional energy reliability.

The facility, named the Chisholm Trail Energy Center, is expected to begin operations in 2029. Evergy's chairman and CEO, David Campbell, described the project as a crucial infrastructure investment to meet the growing energy demands of the region. This marks the first new baseload power plant added to Evergy's Kansas generation fleet in over four decades.

The Chisholm Trail Energy Center is strategically located in northwest Sumner County and will utilize advanced combined-cycle technology. This modern approach is designed not only to provide a reliable and steady supply of electricity but also to adapt its output based on the availability of renewable energy sources like wind and solar, thereby ensuring competitiveness in electricity pricing.

In addition to meeting immediate energy needs, the plant is projected to create approximately 500 jobs during its construction phase and maintain 20 to 40 long-term positions, with salaries exceeding $90,000 annually. The investment is expected to generate over $500 million in property tax revenues for Sumner County, further supporting local economic growth.

Evergy continues to expand its energy portfolio, which currently incorporates coal, natural gas, nuclear, wind, and solar generation. The Chisholm Trail Energy Center is part of a broader strategy to enhance energy infrastructure in Kansas, ensuring that both families and businesses have access to reliable and affordable power well into the future.

May 08 2026: Evergy Inc. Projects 8% Growth in Retail Sales Amid Data Center Boom

Evergy Inc. is forecasting retail sales growth of 7% to 8% annually through 2030, a revision from its previous estimate of 6%. This optimistic outlook is largely attributed to the expansion of data centers in its service area. The announcement was made during a quarterly earnings conference call on 8th May 2026, where company officials discussed the impact of large-load electric service agreements (ESAs) on their revenue.

During the call, Evergy's chairman, president, and CEO, David Campbell, revealed that the utility has signed a new ESA with a yet-to-be-named customer. This brings the total demand from large load ESAs to 2.5 GW, a significant increase from the 1.9 GW disclosed in the previous quarter. Campbell also indicated that the company is in talks to secure at least one more ESA with a large customer within the year, underlining the growing demand for electricity in the region.

The utility company has already contracted an additional 450 MW with major customers, including Panasonic’s electric vehicle battery manufacturing plant. Furthermore, Campbell noted that discussions are underway to expand existing ESAs by up to 1.5 GW and to bring in 1.5 GW to 3 GW of new large-load customers post-2030. This potential growth reflects the proactive steps Evergy is taking to enhance its infrastructure in response to increased demand.

In the first quarter of 2026, Evergy reported a modest increase in income, rising 21% to $151.5 million, or 64 cents per share, compared to $125 million, or 54 cents per share, in the same period a year prior. Despite flat revenue of $1.4 billion, the company attributed its income growth to a rate increase in Kansas, higher non-regulated energy marketing revenue, and a reduction in tax expenses. However, rising depreciation and maintenance costs were also noted as factors impacting overall profitability.

To accommodate the growing demand, Evergy has adjusted its planned capital investment strategy, now projecting a capital expenditure of $21.6 billion. This entails increasing gas-fired generation in Missouri to 4.7 GW, while scaling back long-term renewable energy plans by over 90%. The revised strategy comes in response to challenges related to renewable development costs and regulatory changes, indicating a shift in focus as the company navigates the evolving landscape of energy demand.

NASDAQ END-OF-MONTH STOCK REPORT

Evergy Inc's (NASDAQ: EVRG) stock price has now climbed $10.85 (or 15.1%) year-to-date (YTD) in 2026 to close at $82.73.

Compared with the NASDAQ-100 Index which has risen 13.1% YTD, this is a relative price increase of 2% for Evergy Inc shareholders.

Evergy Inc is the NASDAQ's largest Publishing company by market cap.

The stock was the highest gainer in the Publishing sector which is up 10.8% in 2026. Evergy Inc's market cap increased by $2.4 billion to $19 billion.

Evergy Inc. (NASDAQ:EVRG) is a leading electricity provider primarily serving Kansas and Missouri, strategically positioned in the power utility sector with an emphasis on renewable energy and clean energy initiatives. Notable for its innovative TerraPower partnership, Evergy focuses on nuclear development projects, such as the Kansas nuclear power plant, enhancing its sustainable energy capabilities. The company's community programs, including the Good Hometown Grant initiative, underscore its commitment to corporate social responsibility. Evergy's long-term competitive strengths are reflected through its robust ecosystem, reliable supply chain, and leadership in power utility services, ensuring sustainable growth and value for investors interested in Evergy stock.

SECTION 5 RECENT NEWS

May 05 2026: Evergy Inc. Directors Re-Elected Amid $2.8B Grid Investment

Evergy Inc. held its Annual Shareholder Meeting on 5th May 2026, where shareholders voted to re-elect all 12 directors, approve the advisory vote on 2025 executive compensation, and ratify Deloitte & Touche as the company’s independent auditor for the upcoming year. Heather Humphrey, Senior Vice President, General Counsel, and Corporate Secretary, noted that the record date for voting was 2nd March 2026 and confirmed a quorum was present for the meeting.

During the meeting, Humphrey reported on the voting results, stating that all director nominees were successfully elected, and the executive compensation proposal was approved. Additionally, the appointment of Deloitte & Touche was ratified. The re-election of the board comes as Evergy moves forward with significant investments in infrastructure, aimed at improving both reliability and service for its customers across Kansas and Missouri.

CEO David Campbell highlighted that Evergy invested $2.8 billion in 2025 to modernize its grid and enhance reliability, capitalizing on a burgeoning demand from advanced manufacturing sectors and data centers. He noted that the economic development pipeline has expanded to over 15 gigawatts, supported by new electric service agreements with major data center clients totaling 1.9 gigawatts.

Campbell also discussed Evergy's plans for growth through the construction of three natural gas plants and three solar farms, which will add nearly 2.2 gigawatts of capacity. These projects have received the necessary approvals from state regulatory bodies and are complemented by new large load power service tariffs aimed at ensuring cost recovery and affordability for existing customers. The recent passage of Missouri's Senate Bill 4 further supports Evergy's infrastructure investment strategy.

As part of its commitment to returning value to shareholders, Evergy announced a 4% increase in its annual dividend, raising it to $2.78 per share. Campbell emphasized that the company remains focused on translating opportunities into growth while maintaining a strong commitment to customer service, reliability, and safety. The meeting concluded with an invitation for investors to participate in the upcoming first-quarter earnings call, where further details on financial performance and strategic initiatives will be discussed.

SECTION 10 CORPORATE PROFILE

10.1 Activities

Evergy Inc., headquartered in Kansas, is a prominent electric utility company serving approximately 1.6 million customers across Kansas and Missouri. Its core business centers on the generation, transmission, distribution, and sale of electricity through a diversified energy portfolio that includes coal, natural gas, nuclear, and renewable sources. The company plays a vital role in regional energy reliability and sustainability, with a strong emphasis on modernizing its infrastructure to enhance efficiency and reduce emissions. Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy initiatives, particularly wind and solar projects, which have expanded rapidly due to increasing demand for clean power and supportive regulatory policies. This strategic focus positions Evergy to capitalize on the transition to a low-carbon economy, supporting long-term growth and innovation in the utility sector. It is the NASDAQ's largest Publishing company by market capitalisation.

Evergy Inc (NASDAQ:EVRG) strategically positions itself as a leading utility in grid modernization with a formidable $2.8B grid spend. The company's focus on Evergy grid investment and Evergy data center demand underscores its commitment to infrastructure improvements. The Evergy CEO highlights the company's robust plan for Evergy capital expenditure, aimed at enhancing Evergy grid resilience through smart grid solutions. Evergy's infrastructure projects, including transmission upgrades and distribution upgrades, ensure grid reliability while fostering Evergy renewable energy integration. The firm capitalizes on Evergy commercial data center solutions by supporting Evergy data center power needs and interconnections. This is reflected in their growing Evergy data center load growth. Moreover, Evergy's utility infrastructure spending catalyzes Evergy rate base growth, enhancing shareholder value. Regular EVRG investor updates, where shareholders re-elect directors and approve pay, emphasize strong corporate governance. Evergy investor relations and regulated utility investments secure its position as a notable Evergy utility stock EVRG in the market.

Past Week: Weekly: Evergy Inc (NASDAQ:EVRG) ticks down 1.0% on firm volume

Evergy Inc (NASDAQ:EVRG $81.78), the NASDAQ's largest Publishing company by market cap, has ticked down 83c (or 1.0%) in the past week to close at $81.78.

In the past week the market cap has declined by $190.9 million to $18.8 billion.

The volume was 1.2 times average trading per week of 10.6 million shares.

Activities

Evergy Inc., headquartered in Kansas, is a prominent electric utility company serving approximately 1.6 million customers across Kansas and Missouri. Its core business centers on the generation, transmission, distribution, and sale of electricity through a diversified energy portfolio that includes coal, natural gas, nuclear, and renewable sources. The company plays a vital role in regional energy reliability and sustainability, with a strong emphasis on modernizing its infrastructure to enhance efficiency and reduce emissions. Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy initiatives, particularly wind and solar projects, which have expanded rapidly due to increasing demand for clean power and supportive regulatory policies. This strategic focus positions Evergy to capitalize on the transition to a low-carbon economy, supporting long-term growth and innovation in the utility sector. It is the NASDAQ's largest Publishing company by market capitalisation.

Evergy Inc (NASDAQ:EVRG) strategically positions itself as a leading utility in grid modernization with a formidable $2.8B grid spend. The company's focus on Evergy grid investment and Evergy data center demand underscores its commitment to infrastructure improvements. The Evergy CEO highlights the company's robust plan for Evergy capital expenditure, aimed at enhancing Evergy grid resilience through smart grid solutions. Evergy's infrastructure projects, including transmission upgrades and distribution upgrades, ensure grid reliability while fostering Evergy renewable energy integration. The firm capitalizes on Evergy commercial data center solutions by supporting Evergy data center power needs and interconnections. This is reflected in their growing Evergy data center load growth. Moreover, Evergy's utility infrastructure spending catalyzes Evergy rate base growth, enhancing shareholder value. Regular EVRG investor updates, where shareholders re-elect directors and approve pay, emphasize strong corporate governance. Evergy investor relations and regulated utility investments secure its position as a notable Evergy utility stock EVRG in the market.

NASDAQ END-OF-DAY STOCK REPORT

www.buysellsignals.com

Evergy Inc's (NASDAQ: EVRG $82.69) stock price climbed $1.70 (2.1%) from its previous trading session to close at $82.69.

Compared with the NASDAQ-100 Index which fell 35.2 points (0.1%) in the day, the relative price increase was 2.2%.

The 200-day Moving Average Price (MAP) is $74.27, up from $74.22 on Wednesday. The price to MAP ratio is 1.11. In the last 3 months the share price has exceeded MAP on 61/63 days; another bullish signal.

Today its volatility (highest price minus lowest price/lowest price) of 2.3% was 1.4 times its average daily volatility of 1.6%. Today the stock traded at its six-day high of $83.30. The low for the day was $81.39.

There were 2,981,223 shares worth $246.5 million traded today. Thursday's price rise was propped up by firm volume of 1.4 times the average daily volume of 2.2 million shares.

Evergy Inc. (NASDAQ:EVRG) is a leading electricity provider primarily serving Kansas and Missouri, strategically positioned in the power utility sector with an emphasis on renewable energy and clean energy initiatives. Notable for its innovative TerraPower partnership, Evergy focuses on nuclear development projects, such as the Kansas nuclear power plant, enhancing its sustainable energy capabilities. The company's community programs, including the Good Hometown Grant initiative, underscore its commitment to corporate social responsibility. Evergy's long-term competitive strengths are reflected through its robust ecosystem, reliable supply chain, and leadership in power utility services, ensuring sustainable growth and value for investors interested in Evergy stock.

2.1 Activities

Evergy Inc., headquartered in Kansas, is a prominent electric utility company serving approximately 1.6 million customers across Kansas and Missouri. Its core business centers on the generation, transmission, distribution, and sale of electricity through a diversified energy portfolio that includes coal, natural gas, nuclear, and renewable sources. The company plays a vital role in regional energy reliability and sustainability, with a strong emphasis on modernizing its infrastructure to enhance efficiency and reduce emissions. Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy initiatives, particularly wind and solar projects, which have expanded rapidly due to increasing demand for clean power and supportive regulatory policies. This strategic focus positions Evergy to capitalize on the transition to a low-carbon economy, supporting long-term growth and innovation in the utility sector. It is the NASDAQ's largest Publishing company by market capitalisation.

Evergy Inc (NASDAQ:EVRG) strategically positions itself as a leading utility in grid modernization with a formidable $2.8B grid spend. The company's focus on Evergy grid investment and Evergy data center demand underscores its commitment to infrastructure improvements. The Evergy CEO highlights the company's robust plan for Evergy capital expenditure, aimed at enhancing Evergy grid resilience through smart grid solutions. Evergy's infrastructure projects, including transmission upgrades and distribution upgrades, ensure grid reliability while fostering Evergy renewable energy integration. The firm capitalizes on Evergy commercial data center solutions by supporting Evergy data center power needs and interconnections. This is reflected in their growing Evergy data center load growth. Moreover, Evergy's utility infrastructure spending catalyzes Evergy rate base growth, enhancing shareholder value. SECTION 3 RECENT NEWS, PAST YEAR TOP 10 STORIES & PAST MONTH ECONOMIC NEWS

3.1 RECENT NEWS

Recent News 1 of 2, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 338 words

Tue May 5, 2026: Evergy Inc. Directors Re-Elected Amid $2.8B Grid Investment

Evergy Inc. held its Annual Shareholder Meeting on 5th May 2026, where shareholders voted to re-elect all 12 directors, approve the advisory vote on 2025 executive compensation, and ratify Deloitte & Touche as the company's independent auditor for the upcoming year. Heather Humphrey, Senior Vice President, General Counsel, and Corporate Secretary, noted that the record date for voting was 2nd March 2026 and confirmed a quorum was present for the meeting.

During the meeting, Humphrey reported on the voting results, stating that all director nominees were successfully elected, and the executive compensation proposal was approved. Additionally, the appointment of Deloitte & Touche was ratified. The re-election of the board comes as Evergy moves forward with significant investments in infrastructure, aimed at improving both reliability and service for its customers across Kansas and Missouri.

CEO David Campbell highlighted that Evergy invested $2.8 billion in 2025 to modernize its grid and enhance reliability, capitalizing on a burgeoning demand from advanced manufacturing sectors and data centers. He noted that the economic development pipeline has expanded to over 15 gigawatts, supported by new electric service agreements with major data center clients totaling 1.9 gigawatts.

Campbell also discussed Evergy's plans for growth through the construction of three natural gas plants and three solar farms, which will add nearly 2.2 gigawatts of capacity. These projects have received the necessary approvals from state regulatory bodies and are complemented by new large load power service tariffs aimed at ensuring cost recovery and affordability for existing customers. The recent passage of Missouri's Senate Bill 4 further supports Evergy's infrastructure investment strategy.

As part of its commitment to returning value to shareholders, Evergy announced a 4% increase in its annual dividend, raising it to $2.78 per share. Campbell emphasized that the company remains focused on translating opportunities into growth while maintaining a strong commitment to customer service, reliability, and safety. The meeting concluded with an invitation for investors to participate in the upcoming first-quarter earnings call, where further details on financial performance and strategic initiatives will be discussed.

Recent News 2 of 2, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 381 words

Thu Apr 9, 2026: Evergy Inc Reaches All-Time High as Investors Show Confidence

Evergy Inc has reached a notable milestone, achieving an all-time high of 85.25 USD, trading just 1% below its 52-week high of $85.23. This significant moment for the company was marked on 10th April 2026, with the stock's performance over the past year showcasing an impressive 1-year total return of nearly 33%. The utility company's strong dividend track record, having raised its dividend for 22 consecutive years while currently offering a 3.3% yield, further enhances its appeal to investors. However, recent analyses suggest that the stock may be overvalued relative to its fair value, placing it among considerations for the most overvalued stocks list. Evergy's stock price growth reflects both investor confidence and its robust market position, emphasizing the company's ability to capitalize on the evolving energy landscape.

In other recent news, Evergy Inc. reported a Q4 2025 earnings per share (EPS) of $0.42, which fell short of the expected $0.54, marking a 22.22% negative surprise. Despite this earnings miss, investor sentiment appeared positive, as reflected in the stock's pre-market activity. Additionally, Evergy announced the issuance of $350 million in 4.250% notes due 2029, following an underwriting agreement with several major financial institutions. This strategic financial move is part of the company's broader efforts to maintain stability and foster growth.

Mizuho recently raised its price target for Evergy shares to $82, citing the company's signed data center contracts and an expanded five-year capital plan of $21.6 billion. The increase in the rate base growth from 8.5% to 11.5% supports future earnings projections, reinforcing Evergy's commitment to maintaining a solid financial foundation. In addition, BTIG initiated coverage on Evergy with a Buy rating, highlighting the company's potential for 6%-8% earnings per share growth. These developments underscore Evergy's strategic focus on growth and financial stability in the competitive energy market.

The recent stock performance and strategic initiatives by Evergy Inc reflect a company poised for continued success, despite the occasional earnings shortfall. Investors seem optimistic about Evergy's prospects, buoyed by the utility's long-standing commitment to sustainability and energy solutions. As the market evolves, Evergy is well-positioned to leverage its strengths and navigate challenges, making it a company worth watching for potential investment opportunities.

3.2 PAST YEAR TOP 10 STORIES

Story 1 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 80 words

Evergy Forges Advanced Nuclear Partnership With TerraPower and Kansas

Evergy, in partnership with TerraPower and the Kansas Department of Commerce, signed a memorandum of understanding to explore siting a Natrium advanced nuclear reactor in Kansas. The project, backed by significant private financing, aims to add 345-500 MW of carbon-free baseload generation to meet growing industrial demand. While the agreement positions Evergy as a leader in next-generation nuclear technology, it faces substantial risks related to regulatory approval, construction costs, and public acceptance, with a project timeline likely extending beyond 2030.

Story 2 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 71 words

Evergy Unveils $17.5 Billion Capital Investment Program Through 2029

Evergy announced a $17.5 billion five-year capital investment plan to accommodate unprecedented demand growth, largely from data centers. The program allocates funds for new natural gas and solar generation, transmission upgrades, and grid modernization, targeting an 8.5% annualized rate base growth. The plan will be financed through cash flow, debt, and equity, which may dilute existing shares. Execution depends on managing risks like cost inflation, supply chain issues, and regulatory approvals.

Story 3 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 69 words

Kansas Regulators Approve $128 Million Rate Increase Settlement

The Kansas Corporation Commission approved a $128 million annual revenue increase for Evergy Kansas Central, a significant reduction from the initial $196.4 million request. The settlement, reached with multiple stakeholders, balances infrastructure investment needs with customer affordability, resulting in an average monthly bill increase of $8.47 for residential customers. This outcome provides regulatory clarity and facilitates cost recovery for grid modernization projects, demonstrating a constructive but disciplined regulatory environment.

Story 4 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 68 words

Regulators Approve Landmark Tariffs for Data Center Power Supply

Regulators in Missouri and Kansas approved specialized tariffs for large-load customers like data centers, removing a key barrier to securing contracts for new projects. The tariffs establish rates 7-10% above existing industrial prices, require long-term commitments, and hold customers responsible for their interconnection costs. The structure ensures existing ratepayers are not subsidizing new growth and includes clean energy options, making Evergy's service area more attractive for hyperscale development.

Story 5 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 66 words

Evergy Reports Q3 Results, Raises Quarterly Dividend 4 Percent

Evergy reported third-quarter GAAP earnings of $2.03 per share and announced a 4% quarterly dividend increase to $0.6950 per share. Despite 2% weather-normalized demand growth, mild weather conditions led management to narrow its full-year 2025 EPS guidance to $3.92-$4.02. The results reflect the conflict between strong underlying demand and headwinds from interest expenses and regulatory lag, while the dividend hike signals confidence in future cash flow.

Story 6 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 74 words

Evergy Secures Regulatory Approval for 2,184 MW Generation Expansion

Kansas and Missouri regulators approved Evergy's plans to add 2,184 MW of new generation capacity, including two combined-cycle gas plants, one simple-cycle gas plant, and a solar facility. These projects, slated for operation between 2026 and 2030, are designed to meet projected load growth and facilitate the retirement of coal plants. The approvals provide regulatory certainty for construction, though the company faces risks related to cost inflation, fuel price volatility, and supply chain constraints.

Story 7 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 74 words

Lambda Announces $500 Million AI Infrastructure Investment in Evergy Territory

AI cloud provider Lambda announced a $500 million investment to develop an AI Factory in Kansas City, Missouri, repurposing a dormant data center. The facility will launch in 2026 with 24 MW of capacity, scaling beyond 100 MW and featuring high-density NVIDIA GPUs. This project validates Evergy's strategy to attract large-load customers and strengthens the case for its significant capital investments, diversifying its data center client base beyond existing commitments from Meta and Google.

Story 8 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 71 words

Evergy Economic Development Pipeline Surges Past 15 Gigawatts

Evergy's economic development pipeline has grown to over 15 GW, with 4-6 GW of high-probability large-load opportunities, primarily from the data center sector. This potential demand, which could double the utility's current peak load, is the driving force behind its $17.5 billion capital program. While the pipeline validates the company's growth strategy, significant execution risk remains, as converting these opportunities into energized loads depends on navigating complex development and regulatory stages.

Story 9 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 65 words

Evergy Deploys Advanced eSIM Technology to Enhance Grid Resilience

Evergy is deploying Kigen's eSIM technology to improve grid resilience by enabling secure, automated failover between its private LTE network and public cellular networks. This cybersecurity-focused initiative supports grid modernization by ensuring continuous connectivity for smart meters and IoT devices. The technology enhances reliability and supports advanced applications like dynamic billing, representing a foundational investment in long-term operational capability rather than a near-term earnings driver.

Story 10 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 67 words

Edison Electric Institute Recognizes Evergy for Emergency Response Excellence

Evergy received a national Emergency Response Award from the Edison Electric Institute for its outstanding restoration efforts during a Memorial Day storm and its mutual assistance to AEP Kentucky after Hurricane Helene. The award recognizes operational excellence in restoring power, coordinating resources, and ensuring safety. While the recognition has limited direct financial impact, it validates the utility's operational competence and strengthens its reputation with regulators and customers.

3.3 ECONOMIC NEWS

ECO NEWS 1 of 2, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 213 words

Apr 22, 2026: Anterix Closes Benton PUD Private Wireless Sale, Expanding Utility Private-LTE Adoption Base Relevant to Evergy's Grid Modernization Plan

On April 21, 2026, Anterix Inc., through PDV Spectrum Holding Company, LLC, entered into a spectrum license sale agreement with Public Utility District No. 1 of Benton County for a 10 MHz 900 MHz broadband license in Washington state, valued at $771,000. According to industry reports, the utility-owned network will serve more than 58,000 customers across a 927-square-mile territory.

Market data indicates the transaction represents the first private wireless broadband deployment among publicly owned utilities in the Pacific Northwest. The deal expands the national footprint of 900 MHz private-LTE utility networks.

Impact on Evergy: EVRG

Following this development, Evergy gains further industry evidence supporting its existing Anterix 900 MHz spectrum agreement and private LTE deployment strategy across Kansas and Missouri. Operational metrics indicate Evergy serves approximately 1.7 million customers and has identified private wireless as foundational to its Sustainability Transformation Plan, which includes advanced metering, distribution automation, and distributed energy resource integration.

Analysis shows the Benton PUD agreement adds another deployed utility to the 900 MHz ecosystem, reinforcing vendor scale and standardized integration practices relevant to Evergy's rollout. The growing deployment base also strengthens the regulatory record before Kansas and Missouri commissions overseeing capital recovery.

ECO NEWS 2 of 2, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 414 words

Apr 14, 2026: Oracle Names Evergy as Customer in AI Utilities Suite Launch, Positioning the Midwest Utility for Early Adoption of GenAI Operational Tools

Oracle Corporation unveiled a comprehensive expansion of its Utilities Industry Suite on April 13, 2026, at the Oracle Customer Edge Summit in Austin, Texas, introducing artificial intelligence and generative AI capabilities designed to modernize utility operations at scale. New tools include GenAI Asset Summarization, AI-powered meter data management, customer affordability solutions, and advanced grid and Distributed Energy Resource management. Oracle's Opower platform, which serves 45 million North American households and has delivered $4.3 billion in cumulative energy bill savings, anchors the expanded offering.

The launch reflects Oracle's strategy of embedding large language model capabilities directly into sector-specific utility workflows, positioning the company as a vertically integrated provider across customer engagement, operational intelligence, asset management, and grid infrastructure. Oracle's identification of specific customer references at the Customer Edge Summit signals the commercial maturity of its expanded platform and highlights utilities that have already committed to adoption of next-generation AI-powered operational tools.

The inclusion of named customers in the April 2026 announcement reflects Oracle's broader commercial strategy of using early adopters as reference deployments to accelerate platform evaluation among peer utilities. Mid-sized regulated utilities with active renewable energy integration programs and aging infrastructure asset management requirements represent a core segment of Oracle's target market for the expanded suite.

Impact on Evergy: EVRG

Following this news, Evergy - identified as a named Oracle customer in the April 2026 announcement - is among the first utilities positioned to adopt and operationalize newly launched AI tools, including GenAI Asset Summarization and AI-powered meter data management. For a mid-sized regulated utility serving Kansas and Missouri, these capabilities address practical operational challenges including aging infrastructure asset management and DER integration as the company expands its renewable portfolio.

Evergy's named inclusion also reflects its role as a reference customer, which may influence procurement decisions among peer utilities evaluating Oracle's expanded platform. This positioning reinforces Evergy's technology modernization posture with state regulators in Kansas and Missouri, where demonstrating commitment to operational innovation may support regulatory relationships and rate case positioning.

The operational relevance of Oracle's expanded suite to Evergy's specific challenges - aging transmission and distribution infrastructure, growing renewable energy integration, and regulatory compliance requirements - reflects a well-aligned deployment context. Evergy's early adoption status positions it to realize operational benefits from the new AI tools ahead of peer utilities while contributing deployment experience that may inform Oracle's broader utility market rollout.

SECTION 4 RECENT NEWS

Recent News 1 of 1, Source: HourlyBusinessNews

May 05 2026: Evergy Inc. Directors Re-Elected Amid $2.8B Grid Investment

Evergy Inc. held its Annual Shareholder Meeting on 5th May 2026, where shareholders voted to re-elect all 12 directors, approve the advisory vote on 2025 executive compensation, and ratify Deloitte & Touche as the company’s independent auditor for the upcoming year. Heather Humphrey, Senior Vice President, General Counsel, and Corporate Secretary, noted that the record date for voting was 2nd March 2026 and confirmed a quorum was present for the meeting.

During the meeting, Humphrey reported on the voting results, stating that all director nominees were successfully elected, and the executive compensation proposal was approved. Additionally, the appointment of Deloitte & Touche was ratified. The re-election of the board comes as Evergy moves forward with significant investments in infrastructure, aimed at improving both reliability and service for its customers across Kansas and Missouri.

CEO David Campbell highlighted that Evergy invested $2.8 billion in 2025 to modernize its grid and enhance reliability, capitalizing on a burgeoning demand from advanced manufacturing sectors and data centers. He noted that the economic development pipeline has expanded to over 15 gigawatts, supported by new electric service agreements with major data center clients totaling 1.9 gigawatts.

Campbell also discussed Evergy's plans for growth through the construction of three natural gas plants and three solar farms, which will add nearly 2.2 gigawatts of capacity. These projects have received the necessary approvals from state regulatory bodies and are complemented by new large load power service tariffs aimed at ensuring cost recovery and affordability for existing customers. The recent passage of Missouri's Senate Bill 4 further supports Evergy's infrastructure investment strategy.

As part of its commitment to returning value to shareholders, Evergy announced a 4% increase in its annual dividend, raising it to $2.78 per share. Campbell emphasized that the company remains focused on translating opportunities into growth while maintaining a strong commitment to customer service, reliability, and safety. The meeting concluded with an invitation for investors to participate in the upcoming first-quarter earnings call, where further details on financial performance and strategic initiatives will be discussed.

Evergy Inc. (NASDAQ:EVRG) is a leading electricity provider primarily serving Kansas and Missouri, strategically positioned in the power utility sector with an emphasis on renewable energy and clean energy initiatives. Notable for its innovative TerraPower partnership, Evergy focuses on nuclear development projects, such as the Kansas nuclear power plant, enhancing its sustainable energy capabilities. The company's community programs, including the Good Hometown Grant initiative, underscore its commitment to corporate social responsibility. Evergy's long-term competitive strengths are reflected through its robust ecosystem, reliable supply chain, and leadership in power utility services, ensuring sustainable growth and value for investors interested in Evergy stock.
